steps.title: Write the odd integers
steps.content: The four consecutive odd integers, from least to greatest, are $x$, $x+2$, $x+4$, and $x+6$.

steps.title: Translate the inequality
steps.content:
The third odd integer is $x+4$, and $42$ less than the sum of the first and fourth is $x+(x+6)-42$. Therefore,
$$
24(x+4)\le x+(x+6)-42
$$

steps.title: Solve the inequality
steps.content: Simplify: $$ 24x+96\le 2x-36 $$ $$ 22x\le -132 $$ $$ x\le -6 $$ The greatest odd integer that is at most $-6$ is $-7$. The answer is -7.
